reasoning: The NFL owners will meet on May 20-21, 2025, which gives them the opportunity to vote on rules before the June 1 resolution date. While the original proposal to ban the "Tush Push" was tabled in April due to roughly 16 owners opposing it, credible reporters (Mark Maske of the Washington Post) have indicated that a revised, more expansive proposal to ban pushing/pulling any ballcarrier everywhere on the field will be considered. Maske explicitly reports this revised proposal 'is expected to have a reasonably good chance of being approved,' implying that the league office and the Packers have worked to secure the necessary 24 votes (75% supermajority). Furthermore, Adam Schefter reportedly noted that the Eagles are already preparing for the play's removal, and Commissioner Goodell has voiced personal opposition. While flipping 8 votes is non-trivial, NFL rules that are officially tabled to be 'reworked' for the next meeting and explicitly backed by the league office have a high passage rate. I estimate a 76% chance of the ban passing before June 1.
